metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch
metadata.audit_context: DPLAN-0191: Full ownership hardening. All handler wiring verified against .aipass/hooks.json + engine.jsonl firing evidence. Dynamic dispatch via engine._run_handler (importlib.import_module + getattr) means handlers are never statically imported — seedgo's dead_code/unused_function checks are false positives for this architecture.

bypass.reason: All 15 handler files under apps/handlers/ are invoked dynamically by engine._run_handler (engine.py:60-66) via importlib.import_module + getattr on handler path strings from .aipass/hooks.json. They are never statically imported by design. Each handler verified wired in hooks.json AND fired in engine.jsonl (DPLAN-0191). Follow-up for @seedgo: teach dead_code checker about importlib dynamic dispatch patterns.
Bridge called externally by provider settings subprocess — no internal import. Verified wired in ~/.claude/settings.json hook entries.
main() called as subprocess entry point from provider settings — never statically imported.
Bridges import engine module by design — that is their entire purpose.
Thin entry point, no JSON operations to log.
Bridge importing engine module is its architectural purpose.
Bridge imports engine module by design — sole purpose.

Invoked dynamically by engine via importlib from hooks.json handler path 'aipass.hooks.apps.handlers.prompt.identity.handle' — not statically imported by design. Verified wired in UserPromptSubmit.identity_injector + fires in engine.jsonl.
handle() called dynamically by engine._run_handler via importlib.import_module + getattr from hooks.json. Verified fires in engine.jsonl (UserPromptSubmit.identity_injector).
Uses stdlib json.loads to read passport.json — no JSON file ops needing json_handler.

Invoked dynamically by engine via importlib from hooks.json handler path 'aipass.hooks.apps.handlers.prompt.branch_loader.handle' — not statically imported by design. Verified wired in UserPromptSubmit.branch_prompt + fires in engine.jsonl.
handle() called dynamically by engine._run_handler via importlib.import_module + getattr from hooks.json. Verified fires in engine.jsonl (UserPromptSubmit.branch_prompt).
No JSON operations — reads markdown files and outputs text.

No JSON operations — reads markdown file and outputs text.

NamedTemporaryFile creates binary wav for Piper TTS — encoding not applicable to binary audio.

Invoked dynamically by engine via importlib from hooks.json handler path 'aipass.hooks.apps.handlers.lifecycle.auto_watchdog.handle' — not statically imported by design. Verified wired in PostToolUse.auto_watchdog + fires in engine.jsonl.

Invoked dynamically by engine via importlib from hooks.json handler path 'aipass.hooks.apps.handlers.notification.tool_sound.handle' — not statically imported by design. Verified wired in PreToolUse.tool_use_sound + fires in engine.jsonl.
handle() called dynamically by engine._run_handler via importlib.import_module + getattr from hooks.json. Verified fires in engine.jsonl (PreToolUse.tool_use_sound).

Config loader does $AIPASS_HOME variable expansion before JSON parse — json_handler does not support this.
JSONL append-only diagnostic log — different pattern from branch json_handler storage.
Engine uses JSONL diagnostic logging, not branch json_handler — different purpose.

dispatch() is the engine's core purpose — it IS the module's primary function, not a handler that belongs elsewhere. The engine exists to dispatch; moving dispatch to handlers/ would leave an empty module.
Sound mute toggle — touches /tmp/aipass-hooks-muted flag file only, no JSON operations or json_handler storage.
MUTE_FLAG.unlink() removes a /tmp mute flag file for sound toggle — not a tracked resource or production data deletion. Deliberate user action via 'drone @hooks hooksound on'.
Read-only config viewer — delegates JSON loading to config/loader.py, no direct JSON file ops.
print_introspection() called by drone's discovery system, not internal code.

play() called by handler files (stop_sound.py, announce.py) that are dynamically dispatched via importlib — static analysis cannot trace the call chain from hooks.json → engine → handler → sound.play().

Hooks branch does not use json_handler — has its own JSONL diagnostic logging (diagnostics.py) and stdlib json for hook protocol I/O. json_handler coverage, mock_json_handler fixture, and exception_contracts (create_default_raises, save_invalid_raises, invalid_mode_raises) are all N/A for a hook dispatch engine architecture.
Test fixtures live in tests/, not in the 3-layer apps structure.
Hooks branch does not use json_handler — has its own JSONL logging and stdlib json for hook protocol. mock_json_handler fixture is N/A.
Hooks has no json_handler create_default/save_invalid/invalid_mode patterns — those contracts are N/A for a hook dispatch engine.
Test files live in tests/, not in the 3-layer apps structure.
Tests import handlers directly to test implementation details.
Test methods use descriptive names as documentation per pytest convention.
Test data contains command references as part of test fixtures, not user-facing help.

notes.removed_2026-05-19: Stripped 4 illegitimate bypasses — hooks.py/cli, hooks.py/cli_flags, engine.py/modules, engine.py/introspection. Code fixed to meet standards instead.
notes.dplan_0191_2026-05-28: Added dead_code + unused_function bypasses for all 15 dynamically-dispatched handlers after verifying each is wired in .aipass/hooks.json AND fires in engine.jsonl. Root cause: engine._run_handler (engine.py:60-66) uses importlib.import_module + getattr on hooks.json handler strings — handlers are never statically imported. Follow-up for @seedgo: teach dead_code/unused_function about dynamic importlib dispatch patterns.
